pages.title: The Fortuneteller
pages.wikitext:
{{Removed|update=2.0}} {{Infobox/Quest | name = The Fortuneteller | length = Medium | location = [[Kandon-Beda]] | province = Gavel | level = 97 | npc = Jill | experience = 3900000 | reward = * [[Wealth or Knowledge]] }} '''The Fortuneteller''' is a medium level 97 quest in [[Kandon-Beda]]. ==Preview== [[Jill]] asks you to find a key in an old castle south-east of [[Kandon-Beda]] for access to a treasure. ==Stage 1== » Talk to [[Jill]]. {{ Location | location = [[Kandon-Beda]] | x = 746 | y = 150 | z = -4468 }} '''Dialogue:''' * '''Jill:''' Oh, a Human! We don't see many of your kind around here. * '''Jill:''' I hear you people work for cheap, well I might have a little job for you. * '''Jill:''' I'd go myself, but why would I when I can hire cheap help? * '''Jill:''' Theres a lot of green rock to be made here, so listen up. * '''Jill:''' One of the oldest surviving structures of the islands is rumoured to have the key to a fortune in it. * '''Jill:''' It's the stone castle just east of here, along the separation line. * '''Jill:''' I'm not sure what the "Key" is, but be careful acquiring it, if it was easy, they key would have already been found. * '''Jill:''' I'll be here when you get back. ==Stage 2== » Search for the key in the castle. {{RenderLocation | x = 902 | y = 94 | z = -4412 }} {{HideSpoiler|There is a set of three puzzles where you have to push the emerald block into gold block in order to proceed. Here are the Solutions: '''''The order you move the blocks doesn't matter''''' [[File:FortunetellerPuzzle1.png|300px|First puzzle Solution]] Push Block b to the left (if you face to the closed door)
Push block a to the left, and then forwards [[File:FortunetellerPuzzle2(Step1).png|300px|Second puzzle solution (Step 1)]] Push block a forwards -> right.
Push block b left -> forwards -> right.
Push block c left -> forwards -> right. [[File:FortunetellerPuzzle2(Step2).png|300px|Second puzzle solution (Step 2)]] Push block a (or the block closest to the right edge) backwards -> left -> backwards [[File:FortunetellerPuzzle3.png|300px|Third puzzle solution]] Push block a backwards -> right -> forwards -> left -> forwards -> left.
Push block b left -> backwards -> right -> backwards -> right -> forward -> left.
Push block c backwards -> left. Afterwards the door will open }} {{MobTable}} | [[File:EnslavedVillagerGuard(Sword).png|100px]] | Enslaved Villager Guard
(Sword) | 95 | 8150 | Melee | - | - | - | Sky Castle |- | [[File:EnslavedVillagerGuard(Axe).png|100px]] | Enslaved Villager Guard
(Axe) | 95 | 7350 | Melee | - | - | - | Sky Castle |- | [[File:DrygioniEnchantress.png|100px]] | Drygioni Enchantress | 100 | 130000 | Ranged | Teleport
Heavy Teleport
Meteor | {{Element|water|}}{{Element|thunder|Dam|}}
{{Element|water|Def|}}
{{Element|earth|Weak|}} | {{WynnIcon|prismarine crystals}} Bean Sprouts | Sky Castle |} ==Stage 3== » Return to [[Jill]] in Kandon-Beda with the treasure. '''Dialogue:''' *'''Jill:''' You're back! What did you find? *'''Jill:''' Are these...beans? *'''Jill:''' Is this what you found? No map? No treasure? *'''Jill:''' Peh! What a waste of time. This isn't some Fable kid! If you want to read fairy tales, go to the library. ==Stage 4== » Go to the library in [[Kandon-Beda]]. {{RenderLocation|x=718|z=-4510}} '''''The Fable''''' '''''Chapter I''''' ''Over 1000 years ago, before the great dismantling of the Ahms region, a mighty beast roamed the land.'' ''It was not only a beast of immense power, but also great wisdom. Many would come from far and wide to seek its counsel.'' ''However, after a cataclysmic event, the region shattered, and left the beast lost all hope in villager kind.'' ''For it was the desire of riches that shock the land, and the beast could not tolerate it any longer.'' ''The beast departed the fractured region, leaving the people with all the riches in the world, but no land to enjoy it with.'' ''He departed this land leaving only this advice: Should the greed of this land be in reprimand, seek me out. The seeds of enlightenment should be sewn at the canyon ridge.'' ''Although the location of the "Ridge" has been supposedly found, no such seed has ever grown there.'' ''Leading us to believe that the Ahms region has always been fractured, and that this is just a tale passed down to explain what cannot be explained.'' ''Should you ever want to waste your seeds, the location is [630,147,-4590]'' ''End Of Chapter I.'' ==Stage 5== » Find the beanstalk. {{ Location | x = 630 | y = 147 | z = -4590 }} {{HideSpoiler|Throw in a bean you got from killing the boss in the castle, in the green carpet.|how to grow the beanstalk}} ==Stage 6== » Climb the beanstalk. ==Stage 7== » Speak to the Ancient Dragon. {{ Location | | x = 3916 | y = 149 | z = -3406 }} {{MobTable}} | [[File:MigrantGrook.png|100px]] | Migrant Grook | 90 | 4700 | Passive | - | - | - | Above the clouds |- | [[File:CloudStarling.png|100px]] | Cloud Starling | 94 | 6450 | Melee | - | {{Element|air|Dam|}}
{{Element|air|Weak|}} | - | Above the clouds
From Cloud Starling Nests |- | [[File:CloudStarlingNest.png|100px]] | Cloud Starling Nest | 95 | 1000 | None | - | - | 3 Cloud Starlings | Above the clouds |- | [[File:SkyLadybug.png|100px]] | Sky Ladybug | 95 | 2775 | Protect | - | - | - | Above the clouds |- | [[File:Cumulopuff.png|100px]] | Cumulopuff | 91 | 4900 | Passive | - | {{Element|air|}}{{Element|water|Def|}} | - | Above the clouds |- | [[File:MotherCumulopuff.png|100px]] | Mother Cumulopuff | 97 | 8300 | Ranged | Push | {{Element|air|}}{{Element|water|Dam|}}
{{Element|air|}}{{Element|water|Def|}}
{{Element|thunder|Weak|}} | - | Above the clouds |} '''Dialogue:''' *'''Farcor:''' So... another greedy infested simpleton has sought me out for their own desires. *'''Farcor:''' Sorry to disappoint you, but your journey was for nothing. There is no treasure for you to find here. *'''Farcor:''' If you have found your way here, you must know the story. I left Gavel so many years ago. *'''Farcor:''' I've seen this province's history. The destruction of the Ahms Region, the befalling of the Light Forest's Guardian, and the rampage of the darkness in Gavel's northern forests.. *'''Farcor:''' ..But I see into you, as well. Your senseless rampage across this entire world is sickening. Your good deeds cannot hold a candle to the violent destruction you've inflicted on thousands of beings.. *'''Farcor:''' All of this... for what purpose? Now, enough is enough. Leave this place, and never return. My only offering for you today is this book. Within it lies a choice. I expect you to choose wisely.. ==Tips== *Choosing knowledge grants you access to [[Llevigar's Library]], while choosing wealth grants 2 LE. *There is a Tier 3 [[Loot|Loot Chest]] in the top of the castle exterior. ==Trivia== *Jill may be a reference to Jack & Jill, and, coincidentally, Jack is the name of the boy in Jack and the Beanstalk, which is what this quest is based upon. *Farcor is a reference to the dragon Falkor from the Neverending Story. * While this quest was be removed in [[Version 2.0|2.0]], two secret discoveries were added that have some content of the Fortuneteller still in them, [[Llevigar's Secret Library]] and [[Far Above the Clouds]]. {{Navbox Translations}} [[Category:Quests]]

pages.title: Lizardman Darter
Whirl Weird
Boots Relic
pages.wikitext:
{{Removed|update = 1.17}} {{Infobox/Town | name = | type = shrine | image = [[File:BootsRelic.png|200px]] | caption = The Boots Relic Shrine | xcoordinate = -268 | zcoordinate = -1938 | accesspoints = | minlevel = 25 | biome = Plains | mobs = Relic Guardian, Lv 25 | uses = Relic Shrine | requirements = Touch the Relic Crystal | otherinfo = }} The '''Boots Relic''' is one of thirty [[Relics]] scattered around the province of Wynn and Gavel. It is located within the well hidden Boots Relic Shrine. Collecting it gives one [[Relic Boots]] to the player. ==Location== {{Location |location=Near [[Mt. Wynn]] |x=-268 |z=-1938 }} The Boots Relic entrance is located right next to the path leading into Mt. Wynn. The entrance is set into a small hill. To open the hidden door, enter the room populated by iron golems and press two buttons behind fire. When facing the entrance of the room from the inside, one button is located behind the right fire. The other button is located behind a larger fire set in the wall. Once both buttons are pressed, a small hole in the middle of the floor will have opened. Drop down and follow the path to the Boots Relic. ==Rewards== When you touch the Relic Crystal, you will receive a [[Relic Boots]] as the reward. == Guardians == {{Infobox/Mob | name = Relic Guardian
(Boots Relic) | image = [[File:Mob Wither Skeleton.png|200px]] | type = Wither Skeleton | level = 25 | location = Boots Relic | health = 580 | damage = ? | water = Weakness | fire = Defense, Damage | drops = | noCategory = true }} The Boots Relic is guarded by level 25 Wither Skeletons. It is not recommended to fight these. The entrance to the hidden relic is also guarded by iron golem mobs. [[Category:Relics]]

pages.title: Naga Commander
A Fighting Species
pages.wikitext:
{{Removed}}
{{Infobox/Quest | name = A Fighting Species | length = Medium | location = [[Pre-Light Forest]] | province = Gavel | level = 64 | npc = Darnes | experience = 267500 | emeralds = 1856 | reward = * [[Upgraded Orc Mask]] }} '''A Fighting Species''' is a medium level 64 quest in the [[Pre-Light Forest]]. == Preview == The Villager-Troll feud is raging on as ever. Darnes, a villager wanting peace, sends the player on a 'diplomatic' mission to obtain the Troll Leader, Gorgu's, signage of a treaty. == Stage 1 == » Talk to [[Darnes]] in the villager camp. {{Location | x = -1406 | y = 43 | z = -5073 }} '''Dialogue:''' * '''Darnes:''' This is no place for Wynn natives. This is a Villager-Troll war. * '''Darnes:''' Oh, well. Actually, we do need a human to assist us. It would help us avoid conflict at all. * '''Darnes:''' The filthy but thick-skinned Orcs and their troll brothers have been long standing enemies of the Villagers. * '''Darnes:''' Villagers are not of physical might, we prefer the use of magic. However the Orc's skins are immune to all but the strongest spells. * '''Darnes:''' So, if we can settle our differences peacefully, we will... But Orcs are not the most intelligent creatures, in fact I don't think they even know how to spell Orc. * '''Darnes:''' Nevertheless, they do respect loss and victory. If we are able to get the leader, Gorgu, to sign a peace treaty they will have no choice but to stay peaceful, for now. * '''Darnes:''' Now you see our problem. We are not able to get to Gorgu, he lives deep in the caves of the fortress, protected. * '''Darnes:''' Our best bet is to send someone disguised as an Orc, however our body type and smell will give us away instantly. We need you to go in our stead, Orcs won't know a human's scent too well. * '''Darnes:''' A convincing mask should fool them. So, please bring me [3 Orc Skin] by killing some orcs in the Camps just south of here. We will reward you handsomely if you are able to convince Gorgu to sign the treaty. == Stage 2 == » Bring back 3 [[Orc Skin]] to [[Darnes]] by killing Orcs near the camp. '''Dialogue:''' * '''Darnes:''' Oh! Perfect! Let me craft it... * '''Darnes:''' ... * '''Darnes:''' ... * '''Darnes:''' Ha! A work of art if I say so myself! Alright! You will certainly fool those stupid orcs, and that's for sure! You're going to look like a real orc with this mask! * '''Darnes:''' Remember to grunt and shout about how much you love pig for dinner! This will bring us one step further to ending the Orc war one way or another. * '''Darnes:''' Now, go to Leadin, the Orc fortress, by following the path out of the war camp and past the Orcs. * '''Darnes:''' Then put on the mask, search for the cave and make your way to Gorgu and make him sign the peace treaty. But be careful, Orcs are bad enough, but Gorgu's something else! * '''Darnes:''' Oh, and bring me back the [[Orc Mask]] when you're done too, I would like to keep it! == Stage 3 == » Enter [[Leadin Orc Fort|Leadin]], the Orc’s fortress, wearing the [[Orc Mask]]. {{Location | x = -1310 | y = 57 | z = -4869 }} == Stage 4 == » Find the house with a way leading to a cave in the Orc's fortress. {{HideSpoiler| -1297, 71, -4776 .|Location of House}} '''Dialogue:''' * ''There is a system that is blocking the way... Maybe you can find hints to open it in the tents outside, or near the bridges..'' == Stage 5 == » Find a way to enter in the cave. {{HideSpoiler|First, press the villager button, then press the pig button.|How to open the door}} == Stage 6 == » Find the way leading to [[Gorgu]]’s room in the cave. '''Dialogue:''' * '''Gorgu:''' I SMELL HUMAN FLESH IN MY CAVE! FIND HIM AND KILL HIM! == Stage 7 == » Enter in [[Gorgu]]’s room. '''Dialogue:''' *'''Gorgu:''' I SIGN TREATY IF HUMAN BEAT GORGU! == Stage 8 == » Win the duel versus [[Gorgu]] and talk back to [[Darnes]] with [[Gorgu's Handprint]] and the [[Orc Mask]]. '''Dialogue:''' * '''Darnes:''' You've returned! Do you have the peace treaty? * '''Darnes:''' Unbelievable! I never expected an Orc to actually sign anything! * '''Darnes:''' Well, I guess this hand print on the treaty counts.. I guess they don't use pens. * '''Darnes:''' We will stay stationed here to make sure the Orcs don't start any trouble, but the men will be so relieved we don't have to fight. * '''Darnes:''' As promised, here is the reward. It was part of the war fund, but since there is no war, you may have some! * '''Darnes:''' I know I said I wanted to keep this mask... But it smells hideous, you can take it. I even upgraded it for you! Who knows, you might need it again someday. == Tips == * The answer for the Door Puzzle can be found at the entrance of the cave. *You must have your Orc Mask off to be able to complete the quest otherwise Darnes will not talk to you == Trivia == *Taking off the orc mask before the boss fight will deny you exit. Returning to the hub and trying to exit again will instead teleport you to Ragni. *In a cave at -1296 -4831 there is a NPC named [[Lexas]] who wants you to tell Darnes about him. *This quest has been temporarily removed in the [[Version 1.20|1.20 Gavel Reborn update]]. **It will later be readded as [[All Roads to Peace]] in the [[Version 2.0|2.0 Spellbound update]]. {{Navbox Translations}} [[Category:Quests]]

pages.title: Cheese Merchant
pages.wikitext:
{{Infobox/NPC
|image=[[File:CheeseMerchant.png|275px]]
|xcoordinate=1109
|zcoordinate=-4535
|location=[[Ahmsord]]
}}

'''Cheese Merchant''' is an NPC who tries to sell cheese in [[Ahmsord]]. It isn't a normal merchant as the name suggests and it doesn't have a normal shop GUI. After giving 12 [[Emeralds]] to the merchant, they start to get some cheese but give up after a rat that runs away with the cheese.

==Location==
{{Location|location=[[Ahmsord]]|x=1109|y=174|z=-4535}}
Cheese Merchant is located in the small yellow stand on the top of the [[Ahmsord]]. The best way to reach it is by using [[Elevator]].
{{Location|location=Elevator|x=1105|y=1129|z=-4528}}

==Dialogue==

===First Encounter===
* '''Cheese Merchant:''' Oh! You! You want some cheese?
* '''Cheese Merchant:''' I- I know you do! It's only, uh, [12 Emeralds]!

===Next Encounters===
* One of the following lines of dialogue:
** '''Cheese Merchant:''' Pretty sure I asked for [12 Emeralds].
** '''Cheese Merchant:''' If you don't have enough, I can lowe- wait, no. [12 emeralds] please.
** '''Cheese Merchant:''' I, uh, said it was [12 emeralds].
** '''Cheese Merchant:''' You don't have enough!

===After Paying===
* '''Cheese Merchant:''' Perfect! Let me get that for you!
* '''Cheese Merchant:''' Mh, it was supposed to be here..?
* '''Cheese Merchant:''' Gah!
* '''Cheese Merchant:''' What.
* '''Cheese Merchant:''' ...
* '''Cheese Merchant:''' You know what? I give up. No one here likes cheese anyway.

==Trivia==
* The merchant disappears after the rat incident.

[[Category:NPCs]]

pages.title: Explosive Beast
pages.wikitext:
{{Infobox/Mob |name = Explosive Beast |image = ExplosiveBeast.png |type = Hostile Mob |ai_type = Melee AI |level = 102 |health = 27500 |weakness = water, thunder |damage = fire, earth |defense = earth }} '''Explosive Beasts''' are a [[Hostile Mobs (Silent Expanse)|Hostile Mob]] found in the [[Toxic Wastes]]. ==Combat== Explosive Beasts are a melee enemy that follow the player and attack by coming into contact with them. They have a slow movement speed. Explosive Beasts take more damage from {{e|water}} and {{e|thunder}} attacks, deal {{e|fire}} and {{e|earth}} damage with their own attacks, and take less damage from {{e|earth}} attacks. They have no abilities. ==Drops== Explosive Beasts can drop the following ingredients when defeated: {{MobDrops}} They drop one {{WynnIcon|slime block}} Explosive Heart, an Untradable item used to complete the [[Toxic Bounce]] Secret Discovery. {{MobExtraDrops}} ==Location== Explosive Beasts spawn near the [[Toxic Bounce]] [[Secret Discovery]] in the [[Toxic Wastes]]. {{Location|location=Toxic Bounce|coordinates = 1013, 71, -1025}} == Table data == {{MobTable}} | [[File:ExplosiveBeast.png|100px]] | [[Explosive Beast]] | 102 | 27500 | Melee AI | - | {{e|water|}}{{e|thunder|Weak|}}
{{e|fire|}}{{e|earth|Dam|}}
{{e|earth|Def|}} | {{WynnIcon|slime block}} ''Explosive Heart'' | Toxic Pit at 1013, 71, -1025 |- |} == Navigation == {{Navbox Mobs of the Toxic Wastes}} [[Category:Minibosses]]

pages.title: Chestplate Relic
pages.wikitext:
{{Removed|update = 1.17}} {{Infobox/Town | name = | type = shrine | image = [[File:ChestplateRelic.png|200px]] | caption = The Chestplate Relic Shrine | xcoordinate = 931 | zcoordinate = -2178 | accesspoints = | minlevel = 35 | mobs = Relic Guardian, Lv 35 | uses = Relic Shrine | requirements = Touch the Relic Crystal | otherinfo = }} The '''Chestplate Relic''' was one of thirty [[Relics]] scattered around the province of Wynn and Gavel. It was located within the well hidden Chestplate Relic Shrine. Collecting it gave one [[Relic Chestplate]] to the player. It was removed in the 1.17 update. ==Location== {{Location|location=[[Desert of Almuj]]|x=931|z=-2178}} The Chestplate Relic was located just north of [[Almuj]] at an excavation site. To enter, you had to find the three sandstone spikes with buttons on them and press them. Quickly navigate to the center, where a small opening would have appeared. Dropped down, and the Relic would've been sitting right next to the water. ==Rewards== When you touched the Relic Crystal, you will receive a [[Relic Chestplate]] as the reward. There was a 1/6 chance to receive an Elemental Relic Chestplate. == Guardians == {{Infobox/Mob |name = Relic Guardian
(Chestplate Relic) |image = Mob_Wither_Skeleton.png |damage = |health = |location = Chestplate Relic Shrine |drops = None |level = 35 |type = Wither Skeleton |caption = |noCategory = true }} This relic was guarded by level 35 guardians. Unless you were a reasonably higher level, it was recommended NOT to fight the guardians, as they were fast and strong for their level. ==Video Guide== {{#ev:youtube | jCwW_CAcpWw}} [[Category:Relics]]

The '''Wand Relic''' was one of thirty [[Relics]] scattered around the province of Wynn and Gavel. It was located within the well-hidden Wand Relic Shrine. Collecting it gave one [[Relic Wand]] to the player. The relic was removed alogside all the other relics in the 1.17 update.

==Location==
{{Location
|location=Tree Island
|x=-398
|z=-3833
}}
To access the wand relic, you had to find the dark oak slab in a pile of spruce wood slabs and throw an item into it. Inside the bottom of the tree was a button. Pressing this after throwing an item into the dark oak slab opened a small doorway on the side of the island, past the dark oak slab woodpile. 

==Rewards==
When you touched the Relic Crystal, you would receive a [[Relic Wand]] as a reward.
{{-}}

| image = [[File:Mob_Wither_Skeleton.png]]

The Wand Relic was guarded by level 55 Relic Guardians. These mobs were strong for their level; it was best to avoid them.

'''Erlard''' is a villager standing by the Maiden Tower who assists the player in the quest [[The Maiden Tower]]. He tells the tale of the maiden, and how to enter the tower. 
==Location==
{{Location|location=Maiden Tower|x=-1955|z=-5351}}
Erlard is right by the door to the tower. To get to him from [[Sherk]], follow the road north-east.

pages.title: Thieving Rodents
pages.wikitext:
{{Removed|update = 1.17}}
{{Infobox/Quest
| name = Thieving Rodents
| length = Short
| location = [[Ragni]]
| level = 2
| experience = 80
| emeralds = 20
| npc = Farmer Kelri
| province = Wynn
| nocargo = true
}}
'''Thieving Rodents''' was an easy level 2 quest situated just outside of [[Ragni]]. It was replaced with [[Poisoning the Pest]] in the 1.17 update.

== Preview == 
[[Farmer Kelri]] has lost his wedding ring. In order to avoid conflict with his [[Furious Wife]], [[Farmer Kelri|Kelri]] delegates the player to find his ring for him. After a failed attempt, the player is told by [[Farmer Kelri|Kelri]] to look through the rat hive for his ring. After finding the real ring, the player hands it over to [[Farmer Kelri]].

== Stage 1 ==
» Talk to [[Farmer Kelri]].
{{Location
| location = [[Ragni]]
| x = -756
| y = 67
| z = -1661
}}

'''Dialogue:'''
* '''Farmer Kelri:''' Oh ho ho! A fresh recruit!
* '''Farmer Kelri:''' Good to see the army is starting to grow again! I've been waiting for a bit of help from someone with some backbone!
* '''Farmer Kelri:''' You see, I have a bit of a rat infestation on my farm.
* '''Farmer Kelri:''' Nothing I can't handle myself I assure you! But you see, one of the blighters stole my wedding ring, and [[Furious Wife|my wife]] would kill me if she ever found out.
* '''Farmer Kelri:''' I don't know which one took it, but if she sees me looking for it, she will find out. So could you go thump a few of them and find it?
* '''Farmer Kelri:''' Bring me back [1 Wedding Ring] as soon as you find it! Oh, and, don't tell my wife. If you're able to help me, I'll reward you kindly!

== Stage 2 ==
» Kill Rats until they drop [[A Wedding Ring|a wedding ring]] and return to [[Farmer Kelri]].

'''Dialogue:'''
* '''Farmer Kelri:''' Oh, back so soon? That means you found it!
* '''Farmer Kelri:''' Here, give it to me, and hopefully my wife won't see.
* '''Farmer Kelri:''' Oh...
* '''Farmer Kelri:''' ... This isn't my ring. This is [[A Washer|a washer]]! The dang rodents must be in the nest! They really will eat anything.
* '''Farmer Kelri:''' Maybe you could take a look in their hive for me? I know, it is a horrible place and I've been meaning to close it up.
* '''Farmer Kelri:''' But the chances are the rat we're looking for is in there. Its over in the field, and don't forget to make sure my wife doesn't see you.
* '''Farmer Kelri:''' Bring me [1 Wedding Ring] as soon as you take care of the rat who stole it!
* '''Farmer Kelri:''' The nest is in the cave right over there.

== Stage 3 ==
» Find [[The Wedding Ring|the ring]] in the hive and return to [[Farmer Kelri|Kelri]].
*  The Wedding Ring is inside the Giant Rat at the end of the rat nest in a cave, so you must defeat the giant rat to receive the ring

'''Dialogue:'''
* '''Farmer Kelri:''' Ohh, you found it! Thank the king! I think I might have gotten away with this afterall!
* '''Farmer Kelri:''' Well, thank you very much soldier. Are you looking to head to Detlas soon? Its the hub city of the whole province, everyone goes there!
* '''Farmer Kelri:''' I wanted to go once, but my wife wouldn't let me. You should go! Just follow the emerald trail through the forest.
* '''Farmer Kelri:''' There are dangerous monsters there though, so make sure you are strong enough. Anyway, heres a small reward for helping me out.

== Trivia ==
* This quest was replaced by [[Poisoning the Pest]].

{{Navbox Translations}}

pages.title: Trade Market
pages.wikitext:
{{Infobox/NPC
| name = Trade Market
| image = [[File:TradeMarket.jpg|250px]]
| location = [[Detlas]], [[Troms]], [[Llevigar]], [[Cinfras (City)|Cinfras]], [[Thesead]], [[Rodoroc]], [[Ahmsord]], [[Corkus City]], [[Selchar]], [[Lutho]]
}}

The '''Trade Market''' is a global in-game trading post introduced by [[Supply and Delivery]] that can be used to easily [[Trading|trade]] with other players in Wynncraft. The '''Trade Market''' can be accessed via the NPCs located in most big cities such as [[Detlas]], [[Cinfras (City)|Cinfras]] or [[Lutho]]. 

== Using the Trade Market ==
The Trade Market is used for players to sell items to one another. Items that can be sold in the trade market include

* [[Armour]]
* [[Weapons]]
* [[Crafting Materials]]
* [[Crafting Ingredients]]
* [[Horses]]
* [[Crafting|Crafted Items]]
* [[Dungeon Key]]s
* [[Runes]]
* And more

=== The Main Menu ===
[[File:TradeMarketMenu.png|thumb|250px|The main menu of the Trade Market]]
The main interface of the market displays a list of all the newest available trades. There is a variety of options down the right side which allow for differing functionalities.
*{{WynnIcon|refresh}} The '''Front Page Filter''' allows you to change the level range of the items that display on the front page. This defaults to the level range that your current character is in.
*{{WynnIcon|book and quill}} The '''Reveal Item Names''' option allows you to reveal an un-identified item's name and potential statistics. This takes a fee for each item, however, it also allows un-identified items to be sold on the market.
*[[File:ArrowUp.png|30px]] The '''Back to Page #''' button allows for ascending one page in the main menu.
*[[File:ArrowDown.png|30px]] The '''Forward to Page #''' button allows for descending one page in the main menu.
*{{WynnIcon|compass}} The '''Search Item''' button allows for [[Trade Market#Searching|searching]] using a variety of filters.
*{{WynnIcon|add}} The '''Sell Item''' button opens the menu to [[Trade Market#Listing an Item|list an item on the market]]
*{{WynnIcon|paper}} The '''View Your Trades''' button opens the [[Trade Market#Viewing Your Trades|Trade Overview]] menu which shows you all of your items currently held by the market.

=== Viewing Your Trades ===
[[File:TradeMarketOverview.png|thumb|250px|The trade overview menu of the Trade Market]]
The Trade Overview menu is accessed via the View Your Trades option on the main menu. It shows all of the player's orders on the market, which can be filled, unfilled, or cancelled buy or sell orders. The amount of trade slots depends on the player's [[Ranks|rank]]: 5 slots by default, 10 with VIP+, 15 with HERO, 18 with HERO+ and 21 with CHAMPION.

=== Creating a sell order ===
[[File:TradeMarketSelling.png|thumb|250px|The listing menu of the Trade Market]]
The listing menu can be accessed both from the [[Trade Market#Viewing Your Trades|Trade Overview]] menu or from the [[Trade Market#Main Menu|Main Menu]].
In this menu, items can be listed and sold on the market. To select the item to sell, simply click on it in your inventory. The amount to sell can be selected using the plus icon and the cost per item can be selected via the emerald icon. The trade is listed once the confirmation tick has been pressed. There is also a price check option on the right which shows the Highest Seller, Lowest Buyer and 2 Week Average prices. 

All items placed for sale on the market will present the buyer with a 5% transaction fee. Therefore, the price displayed on the market will be higher than the initial intended price. This transaction tax is not paid to the seller.

=== Searching ===
[[File:TradeMarketSearch.png|thumb|250px|The search menu of the Trade Market]]
The search menu is accessed via an icon on the [[Trade Market#Main Menu|Main Menu]].
It allows for searching and creating a [[Trade Market#Creating a Buy Order|buy order]] for any item that can be bought in the Market. Searching for an item with a specific set of characteristics, including its name, can be achieved using the numerous filters. After you search for an item, you can also sort through the results under several criteria: most/least recent, most/least expensive, and by level.

=== Creating a buy order ===
[[File:TradeMarketBuy.png|thumb|250px|The buy menu of the Trade Market]]

Upon selecting a specific item, the quantity and price to buy the item at can be specified on the buy menu, and a buy order can be opened, which can be filled depending on offerings on the market. This allows you to automatically buy items on the market until you reach the amount of items you want.

== Locations ==
=== Wynn Province ===
There are only two Trade Markets: one in [[Detlas]], the central hub of the Wynn plains, and another in [[Troms]], the main town of the [[Jungle]].

==== Detlas ====
The Trade Market in Detlas is located at the Eastern path of the town, on the path that would lead to the [[Black Road]]. The NPCs are located under a small awning.
{{RenderLocation|location=[[Detlas]]|x=506|z=-1573}}

==== Troms ====
The Trade Market in Troms is located North of the main square where the bank is. The NPCs are up a flight of stairs and under a small shed.
{{RenderLocation|location=[[Troms]]|x=-857|z=-979}}


=== Gavel Province ===
There are a total of five Trade Markets in the Province of Gavel: the first is in the quartz city of [[Llevigar]]; the second is in [[Cinfras (City)|Cinfras]], the central hub of Gavel; the third is in [[Thesead]], the largest city in the [[Canyon of the Lost]]; the fourth is all the way up in the [[Molten Heights]] in the dwarven city of [[Rodoroc]] and the final market is in the floating city of [[Ahmsord]], the heart of the [[Sky Islands]].

==== Llevigar ====
The trade market in Llevigar is on the North-Western side of the bank, close to the stairs that would lead down to the ground floor. The NPCs are located under a small quartz shed.
{{RenderLocation|location=[[Llevigar]]|x=-2010|z=-4491}}

==== Cinfras ====
The trade market in Cinfras is located North of the town's center. (TBA)
{{RenderLocation|location=[[Cinfras]]|x=-455|z=-4948}}

==== Thesead ====
The trade market in Thesead can be found to the left of bank's exit, near the blacksmith.
{{Location|coordinates=816, -5078}}

==== Rodoroc ====
The trade market in Rodoroc is located east of the city's bank and across from the blacksmith. The NPCs are under a similar looking stall as the blacksmith's, but with a blue and red tarp rather than a dark grey one.
{{RenderLocation|location=[[Rodoroc]]|x=1011|z=-5074}}

==== Ahmsord ====
The trade market in Ahmsord is located in the main open-air plaza of the city, west of the large circular building housing the bank and identifier. The NPCs are located under a gazebo with a wooden roof directly next to where you would exit if you were to take the Juggler's Tavern in Cinfras.
{{RenderLocation|location=[[Ahmsord]]|x=1000|z=-4565}}


=== Ocean ===
The only trade market located in the ocean is on the island of [[Selchar]]. The NPCs are located under a blue tent in the island's center, close to the link bridge that connects the main island to the bank
{{RenderLocation|location=[[Selchar]]|x=107|z=-3195}}


=== Corkus Province ===
The only trade market in the [[Corkus Province]] is in the [[Corkus City]]. The NPCs are located north-west of the city center underneath a large blue tent with blue cloth lying all over the ground.
{{RenderLocation|location=[[Corkus City]]|x=-1618|z=-2936}}


=== Silent Expanse ===
The only trade market in the entirety of the [[Silent Expanse]] is in [[Lutho]]. Coming in from the [[Eyeball Forest]] via the southern gate, the NPCs are located on the other side of the Obelisk and bank underneath a black and blue canvas.
{{RenderLocation|location=[[Lutho]]|x=1008|z=-723}}


==Trivia==
* The Trade Market was added in [[Version 1.18|1.18 The Economy Update]]
* Originally the two NPCs served different purposes.

[[Category:Core Mechanics]]

pages.title: Easter Eggs
pages.wikitext:
{{Spoiler}}
'''Easter Eggs''' are hidden jokes or references within the game placed by Builders or GMs to provide fun for players. Some Easter Eggs refer to Wynn lore or future releases, while others hide [[Loot Chests]] or are just locations that are hard to find.

This list covers discovered Easter Eggs and removed or unconfirmed Easter Eggs. Unfortunately, many of the Easter Eggs have been removed, either due to reworks of the area, improvement or reworks of [[Quests]], or just simply removed for no reason.

== Easter Eggs ==
===Ragni===

* There is a secret emerald room on the third floor of the [[Ragni]] bank.
*There is a shower in one of the houses

=== Little Wood ===

* The territory of Little Wood, and the [[:en:The Little Wood Cabin|Little Wood Cabin]] itself, are a reference to the Yogscast member Martyn InTheLittleWood.
* There is a sign inside stating "Gone to travel -Martyn <3".
* There is a song for the reigon called "In The Little Wood".

===Almuj===

* The [[Black Market Merchant]] is hidden behind a waterfall
*a pool of water and a stash of emeralds can be found in the house at 976 83 -1924

=== Ternaves ===
* In the Bovemist Church, Salted's player head can be found on a pedestal.
*On a mountain near Ternaves there is an overpass leading to the Mesa. In it can be found a grave reading "to chime and bell, to solitude. I bid thee, farewell."
*A sign with a Wither Skull renamed 'The' can be found next to one of the wooden pillars.
**There were five other Wither Skulls in Bovemist Churches that formed the message 'The Lord Got Lost In Memories'
**Ternaves is the only church confirmed to still have part of the message present.
***This is due to updates reworking churches that revolve around new content, such as [[Elkurn]] for [[Studying the Corrupt]] and [[Saint's Row]] for the [[Rotten Passage]]

=== Freezing Heights ===
{{Location|x= -1439|y= 9|z= -5406}}
*There is a snowman at the above coords.
[[File:Freezing Heights Snowman.png|250px]]

==Removed/Unconfirmed Easter Eggs==
{{Removed}}

=== Legacy Tutorial ===

* There is a sign behind the carriage which says 'She loves me, she loves me no she loves me but I'm a robot'
* There is a small valley near the Armor Merchant, and it has a wolf called Lonely Wolf, which will follow you.

===Ragni===

* There is a hidden jail within the king's palaces.

===Emerald Trail===
* Below the potion shop can be found the entrance to the [[Sewer Trap]].
===Detlas===
* There is an Anti-Bovemist lair within the city walls.
**While this was rumoured to be true, no evidence from previous versions of the map have shown that there was any lair.

===Ternaves===
* There is an Anti-Bovemist lair below the Bovemist Church.

=== Llevigar ===
* There is a sign that says "I need a hug." at -2013 / 77 / -4519/7 near the [[Bank]].

[[Category:Spoilers]]

pages.wikitext:
{{Removed|update=1.20}}{{Infobox/Items
| image =  {{WynnIcon|Book}}
| item = Book
| type = Quest Item
| obtain = Administration Office
| usage = [[Acquiring Credentials]]
}}
The '''Secret Documents''' are part of the [[Acquiring Credentials]] quest and needed to get into the Black Market.

==Location==
{{Location|location=Administration Office|x=-248|y=66|z=-4999}}
{{Location|location=Basement Door|x=-245|y=59|z=-5004}}
{{Location|location=Secret Documents|x=-105|y=24|z=-6054}}

==Obtain==
In order to get the Secret Documents you have to kill a Security Guard first.

[[File:Security Guard.png|200px]]

The guard will drop a 'Guard Key' that is required in order to enter the Administration Office's basement.

When you got the key go and enter the basement with it.
You'll have to sneak around the guards inside the basement.

After that you'll get to a puzzle.
{{HideSpoiler|
[[File:Acquiring Credentials Riddle Solution.png|200px]]
|the solution of the puzzle}}
Once you did the puzzle you'll be able to get to the room with the Secret Documents.

'''Goblin Scouts''' are a [[Hostile Mobs (Wynn)|Hostile Mob]] found around [[Ragni]].
==Combat==
Goblin Scouts are a ranged enemy that follow the player and try to damage them by firing projectiles at a distance.

They do not have any abilities.

==Drops==
Goblin Scouts can drop the following items when defeated:
{{MobDrops
|{{MDRow|item=Bone Meal|is_guaranteed=0}}
}}
Goblin Scouts may additionally drop [[Normal Items]], [[Unidentified Items]], [[Emeralds]], [[Powders]], and [[Potion of Healing|Potions of Healing]].
==Location==
Goblin Scouts can appear along the portion of the [[Emerald Trail]] closer to the entrance of Ragni. 
{{Location
|location=Emerald Trail
|x=-697 
|y=67
|z=-1587
}}

== Trivia ==

* They are now replaced with the [[Corrupted Scout]] mob.

pages.title: Sister City
pages.wikitext:
{{Removed|update=1.19}}
{{Infobox/Quest
| name = Sister City
| length = Short
| location = [[Detlas]]
| level = 28
| experience = 6000
| emeralds = 128
| nocargo = true
| npc = Lil 
| province = Wynn
}}
'''Sister City''' was a short level 28 [[Quests|quest]] which focused on [[Detlas|Detlas']] and [[Selchar|Selchar's]] "sister" relationship. 

== Preview == 
In this quest the player briefly learns about the Detlas hourglass and the history of Selchar, which is a town in the ocean based off Detlas. Lil explains that the hourglass in the center of Detlas is running out of sand, and it is in need of some extra Hourglass Sand. Lil requests the player to venture to Selchar and bring back some Hourglass Sand.

== Stage 1 ==
» Talk to Lil in Detlas.
{{Location|location=Detlas|x=453|y=67|z=-1562}}

'''Dialogue:'''
*'''Lil:''' I am glad to see you! Detlas needs your help!
*'''Lil:''' You see this giant hourglass? It's losing its sand as we speak. Soon enough, it will be empty!
*'''Lil:''' Fortunately, another city was built based off of Detlas, the ocean city of Selchar.
*'''Lil:''' They have a very similar hourglass. I'm sure they wouldn't mind us taking a bit of their sand, it is magic after all.
*'''Lil:''' It's in the middle of the ocean. You think you could go there for us? Bring me [12 Hourglass Sand].

== Stage 2 ==
» Find 12 Hourglass Sand that appears around the hourglass in Selchar.

{{Location|location=Selchar|x=84|y=68|z=-3141}}

== Stage 3 ==
» Bring to Lil 12 Hourglass Sand.

'''Dialogue:'''
*'''Lil:''' Wonderful! That's exactly what I wanted.
*'''Lil:''' Thank you for your help. Here, take this as payment for your hard work.

== Tips ==
* If you have business beforehand in Selchar, make sure to attain some Hourglass Sand for easy completion of Sister City.
* At [[Nemract]], find the [[Seasail Merchant]] at the docks and buy a Selchar Pass. It can be used to board the [[V.S.S. Seaskipper]] for quick travel to Selchar.
* Find the giant hourglass that looks similar to the one in the center of Detlas, you will find sand lying on the ground around it.

== Trivia ==
* The quest dialogue used to claim that Detlas was built based off of Selchar, however, this conflicted with many other fragments of lore throughout the game and was changed as a result.
* The quest was replaced by [[Misadventure on the Sea]] in [[Version History#1.19|1.19 Silent Expanse Update]].

== Related deleted content ==
=== NPCs ===
* Lil
** Starting NPC of the quest who need the player to get new sand for the clock.

=== Items ===
* Hourglass Sand

=== Trivia ===
* Lil used to be named "Lol". It is unknown whether this was intended or a typo.
* Hourglass Sand was the second self-spawning item introduced.

File:Lil.png|Lil
</gallery>

Farmer '''Laloire''' lives in the [[Kander Forest]]. One day a hole appeared near his farm, and while investigating it, he was deafened from hearing the scream of a Banshee, starting the quest [[Hollow Sirene]].

==Location==
{{Location|location=[[Kander Forest]]|x=-660|z=-5199}}
To find the quiet hidden farm start at [[Cinfras]]. Leave the city through the northern gate and turn left. A farm will appear to your right. Follow the path through the fields to the house. Walk past it to the right. Another farm will appear behind to the northwest. This is where Laloire lives. Just enter the first house.

==Tips==
Close the door behind you so mobs don't disturb you.

Death Oni
pages.wikitext:
{{Removed|update = 1.18}}
{{Infobox/Mob
|name = Death Oni
|image = Mob_Death_Oni.png
|caption = Death Oni
|type = Wither Skeleton
|level = 35
|location = [[Half Moon Island]]
|health = 1149
|damage = 19-21
|thunder = Damage, Defense
|air = Defense
|earth = Weakness
|water = Weakness
|drops = [[Oni Helm]]
}}
The '''Death Oni''' was a level 35 miniboss, which could be found in a cave beneath [[Half Moon Island]]. It looked like a wither skeleton wielding a sword and helmet. It seemed to be the leader and the most powerful of the other mysterious undead mobs found across the island, including the [[Oni Zombie]]s, [[Oni Skeleton]]s and the level 30 [[Oni Shaman]].

On death, the Oni was guaranteed to drop the Rare Level 30 [[Oni Helm]].

==Location==
{{Location|location=[[Half Moon Island]]|x=994|y=5|z=-2634}}
The Death Oni resided in the underground tomb below [[Half Moon Island]]. A maximum of one instance would be alive at any given time, and it would spawn in the open grave at the far end of the room, guarded by many Oni Zombies and Oni Skeletons. A Tier III [[Loot|Loot Chest]] could also be found above the grave where the Oni spawns.

In order to access the Death Oni, the barrier in the underground cavern where it resides (on the south side of the island) had to be opened first. Near the top of the ruined tower complex north-east of the cave entrance, where the [[Oni Shaman]] could be found, was a pressure plate; dropping any item on this plate would temporarily open the Oni's tomb. The door to leave the tower at the top is one-way only, so the entire ruin must be traversed in order to reach the pressure plate.

==Strategy==
When the player was spotted by the Death Oni after entering its chamber, it would rush forward to attack immediately. It fought using its sword in close melee combat, so classes with more range had to stay out of its reach, but it had several methods for closing the distance between itself and opponents; its movement speed was much higher than most other mobs, and it also frequently used the Charge and Pull spells to move around the room very quickly or to force the player within its melee range, respectively.

The Oni had a relatively small amount of health for a boss mob, but its maneuverability made it very hard to avoid damage from it if the player did not have access to effective movement spells. In addition, its undead minions spawned in large numbers throughout the chamber, and could quickly overwhelm the player during the fight with the Oni if the player wasn't careful to avoid leaving too many alive at once.

The Death Oni had decent defenses against the {{Element|air}} and {{Element|thunder}} elements, and it took additional damage from {{Element|earth}} and {{Element|water}} attacks, so equipment based on these elements was very helpful for lower-leveled players.

== Trivia ==
* The Death Oni, like the other Oni mobs across Half Moon Island, was named after a kind of [[Wikipedia:Oni|Japanese demon]].
* The Death Oni used to drop 1-5 {{WynnIcon|emerald block}}Emerald Blocks, but was nerfed due to players making huge amounts of Liquid Emeralds very quickly by farming it.

It was guarded by level 35 Relic Guardians. It was also one of the easiest [[Relics]] to find and could even be seen on the [[map]]. There were a number of Key Merchants located around [[Wynn Province|Wynn]], each selling a Dungeon Key used to enter their corresponding dungeons.

==Usage==
The skeleton key would give access to the Skeleton Dungeon, the Spider Key gave access to the Spider Dungeon, and so on.
Beating each dungeon would give the player the key to the next one, and the Silverfish Dungeon would give you the key to the Skeleton Dungeon, to start them all again. It was possible to skip the Skeleton Dungeon by purchasing the key for the Spider Dungeon. These were replaced with the [[Key Guardian]]s in the [[Version History#1.14.1|1.14.1]] update.

'''Pastor Nitram''' was currently tending the cemetery in the [[Kander Forest]]. During the [[Hollow Sirene]] quest [[Laloire]] sends the Player to him to ask him what he might know about the screams coming from the cave. He tells the Player that it's the scream of a Banshee, and that the player will need special earmuffs to be able to fight it, thus he sends the Player to get them from the tomb of a dead scientist.

Since the [[2023 Festival of the Spirits]] occurred, Pastor Nitram is assumed to be the head pastor of the [[House of Talor]].

== Location ==
{{Location|location=[[Kander Forest]]|x=-620|z=-5351}}
'''Pastor Nitram''' can be found at the entrance to the cemetery in the [[Kander Forest]], right next to the ruined  cathedral.

== Dialogue ==
=== After Quest ===
*'''Pastor Nitram''': May the light return to the forest one day.

'''小偷老鼠'''係一個於Ragni外進行的等級2嘅初級任務。

== 預覽 == 
[[Farmer Kelri|農夫Kelri]]唔小心唔見咗隻結婚戒指。爲免農夫同[[Furious Wife|潑婦(老婆)]]嘅爭執，[[Farmer Kelri|農夫]]拜託玩家幫佢搵戒指。喺一次失敗嘗試後，[[Farmer Kelri|農夫]]再次拜託玩家去鼠穴搵戒指。搵到之後，玩家將佢交俾[[Farmer Kelri|農夫]]。

== 第一階段 ==
» 與[[Farmer Kelri|農夫Kelri]]i對話
{{Location
| location = [[Ragni]]
| x = -756
| y = 67
| z = -1661
}}

'''對話：'''
* '''Kelri:''' 哇哈哈！係新兵啊！
* '''Kelri:''' 真係好啊，軍隊又開始增強啦！我一直都等緊人幫下我手。
* '''Kelri:''' 你睇吓，我個農場有一大堆老鼠出沒。
* '''Kelri:''' 我話俾你聽，我真係攪唔掂！不過咁，其中一隻老鼠偷咗我隻結婚戒指，如果俾果個[[Furious Wife|潑婦]]知道嘅話佢一定會斬我頭！
* '''Kelri:''' 我唔知邊隻攞咗，不過如果佢見係我搵緊戒指嘅話就會俾佢發現㗎啦！所以可唔可以幫手打幾隻老鼠，幫我搵翻隻戒指啊？
* '''Kelri:''' 你搵到嘅時候即刻帶【結婚戒指 Wedding Ring x1】翻嚟俾我啊！啊！同埋千祈唔好同個潑婦講啊。如果你肯幫我嘅話我會俾份厚酬你㗎！

== 第二階段 ==
» 殺掉老鼠直到獲得【[[A Wedding Ring|結婚戒指 Wedding Ring]]】並交給農夫。

'''對話：'''
* '''Kelri:''' 嘩咁快翻嚟嘅？即係你搵到啦！
* '''Kelri:''' 嚟，快啲俾我。希望個潑婦見唔到啦。
* '''Kelri:''' 咦？
* '''Kelri:''' 呢個唔係我個戒指嚟架喎，呢個係洗滌器黎㗎喎。隻死人老鼠一定係喺鼠穴入面啦！佢哋乜嘢都嚼落肚㗎！
* '''Kelri:''' 可唔可以幫我睇吓個穴啊？我知，達地方有真係幾恐怖嘅同埋我都有諗過封住佢。
* '''Kelri:''' 不過宜家我哋要搵嘅老鼠喺個洞穴入面。個穴就喺農場嘅遠處，同埋千祈千祈唔好俾個潑婦見到啊！
* '''Kelri:''' 處理好果隻死人老鼠之後即刻帶【結婚戒指 Wedding Ring x1】翻嚟俾我啊！
* '''Kelri:''' 個巢穴就喺果度喳！

== 第三階段 ==
» 喺鼠穴入面搵翻隻[[The Wedding Ring|戒指]]俾[[Farmer Kelri|農夫]]
*  隻戒指就係喺穴嘅最篤篤隻巨鼠（Giant Rat）嘅肚入面，所以一定要殺死佢先可以獲得隻戒指|尋找戒指

'''對話：'''
* '''Kelri:''' 嘩啊啊！你搵到啦！感謝國王啊啊！！畢竟我竟然可以僥倖過關！
* '''Kelri:''' 啊，唔該曬你啊士兵！你遲啲會唔會去一去Detlas城啊？佢係成個省中軸城市嚟架，全部人都去果度㗎！
* '''Kelri:''' 我有一次想去㗎，不過個死人潑婦唔肯俾我去。你應該要去啊！沿住綠寶石之路（Emerald Trail）走穿森林就得㗎喇
* '''Kelri:''' 不過果度有好多危險嘅怪物，所以要確保自己夠料去應付佢哋啊。無論如何，呢度係我小小嘅獎勵。

== 瑣事 ==
* 果度'''原本'''有個錯誤，會喺你完成任務之後唔俾20個[[Emeralds|綠寶石]]你，不過個錯誤已經修正咗。由@Scrubbie確認。

{{Location
|location=[[Jungle]]
|x=-605
|z=-825
}}
In order to open the hidden entrance, two wooden buttons on the side of the small temple must be found and pressed in quick succession. After this is done, stand on the pressure plate and jump down into the hole in the center. The entrance is only open for a brief moment, so move quickly!

== Rewards ==
When you touched the Relic Crystal, you would receive a [[Relic Spear]] as a reward.
{{-}}

}}

'''Buendo''' was the starting NPC for the quest [[Out of My Mind]]. Not much was known about him, though it could be assumed he was a very unpleasant man, given his actions in the quest. When he was first encountered, he had had his body switched with that of the inventor [[Ariodo]], and used this opportunity to take over his lab.

==Location==
{{Location|location=Troms|x=-802|z=-903}}
Buendo could be found on the top floor of the ''Salted Salmon Inn'' in [[Troms]].

==Trivia==
*Buendo is removed due to the fact the quest was reworked in the 1.16 Corkus Update, and he was no longer needed.

pages.title: From the Bottom
pages.wikitext: {{Infobox/Quest | name = From the Bottom | length = Medium | difficulty = Easy | location = [[Thanos Bazaar]] | province = Gavel | level = 81 | npc = Nakoba | experience = 750,000 | emeralds = 4,096 | reward = * 1 [[Troll Hair]] * 40 [[Platinum Ingot|Platinum Ingots]] }} '''From the Bottom''' is a medium level 81 [[Quests|quest]] and is multi-staged. It takes place in [[Thanos Bazaar]]. == Preview == The player looks around the [[Thanos]] market area and barters one item for another until everyone receives the item they want. NOTE: You'll need to obtain 3 [[Troll Hair]] before starting the quest. They can found east of [[Thanos]] around (430,-5240). ==Stage 1== » Find someone in [[Thanos]] who wants to buy [3 [[Troll Hair]]]. Nakoba is at 191, 6, -5236 Troll Hairs are dropped from Trolls that spawn near [[Thanos]]. [[File:Nakoba.png|256px]] *'''Nakoba:''' Ah, hey there, human! I'm certain you've noticed the bazaar down here. It's a point of pride for the Dwarves- biggest marketplace of all the Dwarf cities! *'''Nakoba:''' Specialty items you can't find anywhere else- All sorts of strange, wonderful, unique things. I've been looking for a few sundry items myself here. *'''Nakoba:''' It's almost all barter, unlike the merchants above that deal in that [[Emeralds|shiny stuff]]. Which is...a little unfortunate for me, in a way. *'''Nakoba:''' You see, I'm a historian by trade, but what I'm really after now is some Troll Hair. See, I really like the sme- er. It's good and sturdy for binding books- wait a second! *'''Nakoba:''' YOU have some? Phenomenal! I can never get it myself- was never much of a fighter. Um, here! Let me trade you that, for...this! *'''Nakoba:''' A Decorative Bottlecap Set! These were hard to come by, but I've got two sets of them anyways. Check them out! You'll learn a lot. Thank you very much, by the way! * ...this is more useless to you than that troll hair. But, Nakoba did say the merchants barter. Maybe you can get something more useful for these. ==Stage 2== » Find someone in [[Thanos]] who wants to buy a [Decorative Bottlecap Collection]. Dobile is at 192, 6, -5219 [[File:Dobile.png|256px]] *'''Dobile:''' Minerals for sale! Minerals for sale! Come get your rare metals! We've got Kanderstone, Wynnic Copper, genuine Almuj Sandstone, real Voidstone from the Sky Islands! *'''Dobile:''' Human, you there! If you're interested in smithing, you've found the right place! ...so long as you like drinking Dwarven drinks. I'm looking for bottlecap sets in exchange for these. *'''Dobile:''' What's that look for? Are you new here or something? Never seen a bottlecap collector before? Or is that just your face? *'''Dobile:''' Wh- That! That's! I! How did you! It...It's in pristine condition! There's not a single cap missing! There's not even any dents from the bottle openers!!! *'''Dobile:''' There's no way you got those in person! You can't possibly understand the significance of what you're holding!! I! NEED! THEM! *'''Dobile:''' Here! My rarest metal! Take them! Take them and give me those bottlecaps! I've got to complete my collection!! Give it! Give it! Give it give it give it give it NOW! *'''Dobile:''' ...er. Ahem. I got carried away there. But...those bottlecaps are from the very first Dogun Festival. It wasn't even held in Courag at the time these were made! *'''Dobile:''' I'd love to talk about them more, but I can't spend too long on the sale, or other customers might go away. But you got a good deal with that Jasplite I gave you! *'''Dobile:''' Come to think of it, some guy wanted that stuff, but he didn't even have a plain ol' sodapop cap on him! I think he's right close by. Maybe you'd have some interest in what you're selling instead. ==Stage 3== » Find someone in [[Thanos]] who wants to buy some [Molten Jasplite Ingots]. Dorroc is at 179, 6, -5228 [[File:Dorroc.png|256px]] *'''Dorroc:''' Hm...a human trader? Well, I've heard of you, but I hope it isn't just the standard fare from you. *'''Dorroc:''' Yes, I've seen quite a few of your type to this bazaar- Using the community forges and smithing tables in all. You seem to have metals in bulk... *'''Dorroc:''' ...but probably ninety-eight out of a hundred of the metal ingots I've seen humans use have been trash quality! Worthless only for practice! I seek only the finest metals, you see. *'''Dorroc:''' ...so, of course, you have to prove me wrong by having MOLTEN JASPLITE?! Oh, you must have gotten that from Dobile. Wouldn't have taken you as a bottlecap collector! *'''Dorroc:''' I've been pestering him for a while about his stock. The metal of the Unseen Blade is very valuable, you see. It's fragile, yes... *'''Dorroc:''' ...but to wield it is to channel the power of Ockar the Unseen Blade himself! The wartime taskmaster who cowed a hundred demons with a mere dagger! *'''Dorroc:''' Sneaking behind the lines of the enemy, braving blistering heat! Remaining as a shadow, and cutting through sheer earth and magma without flinching!! *'''Dorroc:''' ...erm, yes. Anyways! For that, I can give you nothing less than my rarest stock. A Golemlus Power Core. If [[Qira]] found out I had this, I'd be worse off than a dead man. *'''Dorroc:''' She keeps the secrets of those creatures under lock and key- and it must be the ticket to unlocking that particular mystery. Treasure this, human! It is no mere Corkian battery. ==Stage 4== » Find someone in [[Thanos]] who wants to buy a [Golemlus Power Cell]. Lodog is at 159, 6, -5285 [[File:Lodog.png|256px]] *'''Lodog:''' Right chief, what can I do ya for? I got all kinds'a machines here. One of 'em's gotta catch your eye, y'hear? *'''Lodog:''' I jury-rigged this juicer! Put a lemon inside, give it some, heheh, '''juice,''' and you'll get yourself lemon juice in a jiff! *'''Lodog:''' Or how 'bout this coin-shooter? Turn the crank and this'll spit out any coin-sized object like nobody's business. Just, uh, don't aim it at any windows, unless you really hate that window. *'''Lodog:''' ...what? Oh, I see. You think you got somethin' that'll interest ME instead of the other way round. I gotcha. Well, lemme see it a sec. *'''Lodog:''' ...okay. I can't make heads or tails a' this thing. What'd you say it was again? Don't tell me this is some [[Corkus]] gadget from that not-so-[[Legendary Island]]! *'''Lodog:''' ...a... A Golemlus Power Core...? This... hot DOG! Yahaaah, I'd heard about these wacko golem thingies ages ago, I always wanted to take 'em apart! Aw hell, but this is an opportunity! *'''Lodog:''' Gotta say, for something that came from that [[The Qira Hive (Location)|Hive]] it's a lot less purple and black and spidery-all-over than I expected. The complexity...this has gotta be the real deal, but... *'''Lodog:''' Feels like someone else made this. That Qira lady doesn't seem like the handsy type, and this is something like 85% mechanics, 15% magic. Just seems out of character. *'''Lodog:''' Ah, but I'm gettin' away from myself. Tell ya what- prob'ly went through a lotta trouble ta get this, and that means you got guts. You got bravery. You got STONES, human! *'''Lodog:''' So, how 'bout these Ice Drake Scales then? It fits a brave soul like you, it does! Take 'em with pride, there! ==Stage 5== » Find someone in [[Thanos]] who wants to buy the Ice Drake Scales. Mastro is at 157, 6, -5256 [[File:Mastro.png|256px]] *'''Mastro:''' Ardent Magma Crystals for sale! Pure, 100%! Accept no substitutes, unless you're paying in emeralds, in which case kindly substitute...a lot of them!! *'''Mastro:''' Human! You have a keen eye for prices and values! I can tell by that glint in your eye- a real MAN's spirit! Even if you aren't a man, your spirit shines all the same as a man...! *'''Mastro:''' A SALES MAN! YES! Macho and brave! Chiseled and strong! Strong enough to carry 200,000 Liquid Emeralds to trade for this brilliant Ardent Magma Crystal! *'''Mastro:''' ...but hold on a moment. Hold on just one moment. Do I detect...a vein of ICE in that manly aura? A chill, pushing against that spirit of market machismo? *'''Mastro:''' YES, I DO! Human, you have Ice Drake Scales! I needn't see them to be sure! I was right about you from the start- Such burning passion!! *'''Mastro:''' AND SUCH NAIVETE! Let me strengthen your MIND! Ice Drake Scales are indicative of a great challenge having been overcome! A truly macho trophy of accomplishment! *'''Mastro:''' To tame Ice Drakes required UNBREAKABLE will! To overcome the sheerest cold with MACHO HEAT! And to outshine demons of lava and flame with that same determination! Those scales represent such resilience! *'''Mastro:''' And yet...I must wonder. Such a fiery spirit you have- THOSE SCALES HOLD YOU BACK! Your spirit cannot be contained in such a manner! *'''Mastro:''' DISRESPECT YOURSELF NO LONGER! I DEMAND YOU HAND OVER THOSE SCALES! But you will not leave empty-handed, no no, friend! *'''Mastro:''' You have ignited my passion as a salesman, so I shall present you with one of my own greatest accomplishments! For a spirit so WHITE HOT, only an ARDENT MAGMA CRYSTAL is fitting! *'''Mastro:''' YES! Your soul shines brighter than the shimmer of EIGHT-HUNDRED-MILLION EMERALDS! Forever may your spirit BURN, human! ==Stage 6== » Find someone in [[Thanos]] who wants to buy an [Ardent Magma Crystal]. Dohstaj is at 179, 6, -5253 [[File:Dohstaj.png|256px]] *'''Dohstaj:''' ...you have good money on you. *'''Dohstaj:''' ...I heard Mastro shouting. He's...so passionate. *'''Dohstaj:''' ...he's married to his work. Would I were his. *'''Dohstaj:''' ...but business first. Wartime artifacts for sale. *'''Dohstaj:''' ...priciest thing is a Dogun Flameshooter. Practically invaluable. You want it? *'''Dohstaj:''' ...good doing business. *'''Dohstaj:''' ...sigh. What a man he is... ==Stage 7== » Find someone in [[Thanos]] who wants to buy the [Dogun Flameshooter]. Syni is at 158, 5, -5228 [[File:Syni.png|256px]][[File:Mastro.png|256px]] *'''Syni:''' I can't stand this. I can't stand this! SHUT UP, MASTRO! NO ONE CARES ABOUT YOUR SWEATY SPIRIT OR WHATEVER! *'''Mastro:''' MY MACHISMO CANNOT BE CONTAINED! Your aversion only inspires my salesman's spirit! Ardent Magma Crystals! Pure, 100% gold! 200,000 Liquid Emeralds, 80% off! STRONG bargains! *'''Syni:''' AAAGH! Stupid, stupid, stupid Dohstaj! I just need him to fork over that stupid Flameshooter and then I can get away from this tripe but he just! Won't! DO IT! *'''Syni:''' No, look, human! Can't you see the kind of stress I'm under?! I don't care about your Flameshooter, I need to get my hands on Dohstaj's Flameshooter! *'''Syni:''' ... *'''Syni:''' ...wait. Wait wait wait that's Dohstaj's Flameshooter how in the world did you get it what what WHAT! How did you snap him out of his...emotions?! *'''Syni:''' I've been trying to get a hold of that for a week straight! I already have the perfect trade lined up! Please, hear me out! *'''Syni:''' I have here the Scepter of Draani Thunderwill I! Dohstaj only WISHES he could salvage something this juicy! The real, honest-to-goodness staff of the Wartime King! *'''Syni:''' A war relic for a war relic. It's only a fair trade! Just be real careful- a theif's been skulking in the yellow stall by the bank trying to steal it from me. ==Stage 8== » Find someone in [[Thanos]] who wants to buy [Draani Thunderwill's Scepter]. Yobon is behind the stone walls at 185, 6, -5290 The yellow stall should have the person who was trying to steal the scepter, however it does not have anyone inside of it. A notice is placed outside the stall that says the owner of it is out for a lunch break. By the bank there will be a Melon? Merchant. Right-click the wall behind the merchant to encounter the person who wants to buy the scepter. [[File:Yobon.png|256px]] *'''Yobon:''' ...so. I saw you talking with Syni. *'''Yobon:''' Pfft. Thief? I'm an honest man. Just following the old footsteps. *'''Yobon:''' I know you have the Scepter. I'd have thumped you and taken you if you were a Dwarf. *'''Yobon:''' But how 'bout we work out a deal, instead? Take this tablet. *'''Yobon:''' It'll explain everything. Dwarven History...if you can read it. I can. Won't let loose my secrets for free though. *'''Yobon:''' Hand over the Scepter, and I'll hand over the tablet. *'''Yobon:''' I won't translate it for you, but I'm sure there's some egghead who'll get back to you on what it means in...ten years or so. *'''Yobon:''' Good things come to those who wait for others to do the work for 'em, as they say. ==Stage 9== » Find someone in [[Thanos]] who wants to buy your [Eons-Lost Dwarven History Tablet]. Nakoba is at 191, 6, -5236 [[File:Nakoba.png|256px]] *'''Nakoba:''' Well, you've been busy, I see! You've been working this bazaar like a champion! *'''Nakoba:''' I ought to tell my friends up at the topside shops- they really respect traders who know their way around a deal. You should be able to buy and use more items from the armories! *'''Nakoba:''' I... haven't had such luck unfortunately. I think you might've picked the place clean, eheh. Don't suppose you've got one last deal up your sleeve, huh? *'''Nakoba:''' Oh, really? Hah, I was just joking about that, but if you really do have an offer, go ahead and lay it on me! *'''Nakoba:''' ... *'''Nakoba:''' Oh. *'''Nakoba:''' My. *'''Nakoba:''' HELL. *'''Nakoba:''' THAT'S ANCIENT HISTORY! ''FORMATIVE'' ANCIENT HISTORY! *'''Nakoba:''' I recognize those runes, they're ancient Dwarven glyphs! This is slab number three! We already have the first, second, and fourth tablets, my team and I- this would finish the collection! *'''Nakoba:''' I! I! I! Take! TAKE EVERYTHING I HAVE! TAKE IT ALL, I NEED THAT! I NEED TO DISCOVER THOSE ANCIENT SECRETS! *'''Nakoba:''' AH! AHAHAHAHA! THE HISTORY! THE STORIES! THE EVERYTHING! IT'S BETTER THAN THE SMELL OF TROLL HAIR! AHAHAHAAAHAHHAHHAHAAAAAH! == Traded Items == {{WynnIcon|pumpkin seeds}} Decorative Bottlecap Collection: The bottlecaps depict ornate stone faces on the tops. There's small writing on the other side. Seems like a full set. * Obtained from [[Nakoba]] (191, -5237) * Sold to [[Dobile]] {190, -5219) {{ProfessionIcon|molten ingot}} Molten Jasplite Ingot: They have brilliant crimson bands running along their surface. Seems light for a metal. * Obtained from [[Dobile]] * Sold to [[Dorroc]] (180, -5227) {{ItemIcon|redstone lamp}} Golemlus Power Core: The metal is old, but meticulously cared for. It feels like you shouldn't be looking at this. * Obtained from [[Dorroc]] * Sold to [[Lodog]] (161, -5285) {{WynnIcon|prismarine crystals}} Ice Drake Scales: They feel mildly cold to the touch, and shine in the light. There's an attachment for a pin stuck through one of them...? * Obtained from [[Lodog]] * Sold to [[Mastro]] (159, -5255) {{ProfessionIcon|molten gem}} Ardent Magma Crystal: The shine is more brilliant than any gold you've seen before... ...but it's incredibly heavy! * Obtained from [[Mastro]] * Sold to [[Dohstaj]] (178, -5252) {{WeaponIcon|dagger_fire2}} Dogun Flameshooter: The stone comprising this device seems positively ancient... There's evidence of external repairs on it. * Obtained from [[Dohstaj]] * Sold to [[Syni]] (159, -5227) [[File:GildedWand.png|32px]] Draani Thunderwill's Scepter: The craftsmanship is of the highest quality, and it menaces with spikes of gilded obsidian. * Obtained from [[Syni]] * Sold to [[Yobon]] (186, -5290) {{ProfessionIcon|dark paper}} Eons-Lost Dwarven History Tablet: You can't make heads or tails of the glyphs carved into it. It's preserved perfectly. You've definitely talked to someone who wants this already. * Obtained from [[Yobon]] * Sold to [[Nakoba]] == Trivia == *A Thanos Dwarf NPC in the [[Thanos Bazaar]] mentions the best way to make money is to spend it. He is proven to be correct by this quest, as you start from 3 [[Troll Hair|troll hair]] to ending up with 4096 [[emeralds]] and 40 [[Platinum Ingot|platinum ingots]]. *This quest was updated in the [[Version 2.0|2.0]] update. For the old quest, see [[From the Bottom (1.20)]]. *The description of Draani Thunderwill's Scepter is a reference to the game, Dwarf Fortress {{Navbox Translations}} [[Category:Quests]]

'''Corrupted Lumberjacks''' are a [[Hostile Mobs (Wynn)|Hostile Mob]] found in the [[Elkurn Fields]].
==Combat==
Corrupted Lumberjacks are a melee enemy that follow the player and try to damage them by coming into contact with them.

They do not have any abilities.

==Drops==
Corrupted Lumberjacks can drop the following items when defeated:
{{MobDrops
|{{MDRow|item=Wood Snippet|is_guaranteed=0}}
}}
Corrupted Lumberjacks may additionally drop [[Normal Items]], [[Unidentified Items]], [[Emeralds]], [[Powders]], and [[Potion of Healing|Potions of Healing]].
==Location==
Corrupted Lumberjacks can appear in the [[Elkurn Fields]].
{{Location
|location=Elkurn Fields
|x=-245
|y=70
|z=-1217
}}

{{Navbox Mobs of the Elkurn Fields}}

Undead Soldiers take more damage from {{e|air}} attacks and take less damage from {{e|fire}} and {{e|earth}} attacks. They can occasionally cast the Multihit spell, damaging the player and knocking them around if they are struck by it. ==Drops== Undead Soldiers can drop the following items when defeated: {{MobDrops}} {{MobExtraDrops|Undead Soldiers}} ==Location== Undead Soldiers can be found all throughout the [[Detlas Suburbs]] and [[Ancient Nemract]]. {{Location|location=Detlas Suburbs|coordinates = 219, 67, -1585}} {{RenderLocation/Ancient Nemract}} == Table data == {{MobTable}} | [[File:UndeadSoldier.png|100px]] | [[Undead Soldier]] | 15 | 300 | Melee AI | Multihit | {{Element|air|Weak|}}

pages.title: Jeremy
pages.wikitext:
{{Removed|update=1.14}}
{{Infobox/Mob
| image = Jeremy.png
| caption = Jeremy
| type = Sheep
| level = 71
| location = [[Jungle]]
| health = 2048
| damage = None
| drops = Nothing
| noCategory =
}}
'''Jeremy''' was a unique sheep that appeared in a secret cave in the Troms Jungle. He was a normal White [[Sheep]] mob, except for the fact that he was level 71. He was removed in the 1.14 [[Gavel]] update.

==Location==
{{Location|location=Jungle|x=-539|y=48|z=-599}}
Jeremy's hideout was located with a cave in the Jungle. In the middle of the cave was a fountain, and on the western wall by the fountain was a button which opened the door to Jeremy's cave. He could usually be found at the end of it. Jeremy's small cave had a head in an alcove at the end.

==Trivia==
* Jeremy dropped nothing, and only gave about 52 XP.
* Jeremy was a reference to the pet sheep of the player, Samabizzle, who reached Lv 71 when the level cap was 70.  [http://forums.wynncraft.com/threads/anyone-care-to-explain-this-robin.55057/#post-596783]

pages.title: Blue Powder VI Relic
pages.wikitext:
{{Removed|update = 1.17}} {{Infobox/Town | type = shrine | image = [[File:BluePowder2+.png|58px]] | caption = Water Powder VI | mobs = Relic Guardian, Level ? | uses = Relic Shrine | requirements = Touch the Relic Crystal }} The '''Blue Powder VI [[Relic]]''' gave Water [[Powder]] (up to tier VI) upon touch. It was guarded by level 90 Relic Guardians. It was one of the thirty relics you could find in the [[Gavel]] and [[Wynn]] provinces. This relic had a ? hour cooldown. This meant that after receiving a reward from the Blue Powder VI Relic, you would have to wait ? hours before you could get another reward. ==Rewards== {{Infobox/Items |image = [[File:BluePowder2+.png|48px]] |name = Water Powder VI |item = Dark Blue Dye |obtain = Blue Powder V Relic Shrine |usage = |type = Powder |noCategory = true }} When you touched the Relic Crystal, you would receive one of the following rewards: {| class="wikitable" ! Reward ! Chance |- | {{WynnIcon|water powder i}} 1 x Water [[Powder]] I | align="center" | ?% |- | {{WynnIcon|water powder i}} 1 x Water Powder II | align="center" | ?% |- | {{WynnIcon|water powder i}} 1 x Water Powder III | align="center" | ?% |- | {{WynnIcon|water powder iv}} 1 x Water Powder IV | align="center" | ?% |- | {{WynnIcon|water powder iv}} 1 x Water Powder V | align="center" | ?% |- | {{WynnIcon|water powder iv}} 1 x Water Powder VI | align="center" | ?% |} ==Location== {{Location |location=[[Lower Molten Heights]] |x=1236 |y=27 |z=-5508 }} To get to the Blue Powder VI Relic, you could swim up the waterfall and enter the pit. == Guardians == {{Infobox/Mob |name = Relic Guardian
(Blue Powder VI Relic) |image = [[File:Mob_Iron_Golem.png|100px]] |damage = ? |health = ?? |location = Blue Powder VI Relic Shrine |drops = |level = 90 |type = Iron Golem |caption = |noCategory = true }} Blue Powder VI Relic was guarded by level ? Iron Golems. [[Category:Relics]]

The '''Mistake''' is a level 50 mob that is used in the [[An Iron Heart Part I]] quest.

pages.title: Heart of Llevigar
pages.wikitext: {{Infobox/Quest | name = Heart of Llevigar | length = Long | difficulty = Medium | location = [[Llevigar]] | province = Gavel | level = 41 | npc = Zenam | experience = 24,500 | emeralds = 224 | reward = * [[Generator Amulet]] }} '''Heart of Llevigar''' is a long level 41 quest located in [[Llevigar]] and the [[Llevigar Power Plant]]. == Preview == The 'Heart of Llevigar' (Llevigar's power generator) is starting to melt down, and its maintenance workers cannot figure out how to fix it. If it explodes, it will take the whole city with it, so [[Zenam]] frantically asks the player to help fix the Heart. == Stage 1 == » Find [[Zenam]] in the [[Llevigar]] power control center at [-1872, 55, -4593] [[File:Zenam.png|200px|left]] '''Dialogue:''' *'''Zenam:''' Aaah, no no no! This doesn't make any sense, what in the world is going on?! Nothing's working! *'''Zenam:''' Gweh, a visitor? Get out of here! The plant is shut down for maintenance- wait, are you the electromagic expert we're paying for? *'''Zenam:''' You are, right? Right?! Cause we can't wait any longer! This system was supposed to be unbreakable, a flawless design! *'''Zenam:''' We didn't even need to get Corkian consultation on this, we had this system all figured out ourselves, but now nothing's working right! *'''Zenam:''' You're seeing all these sparks and hearing all this crackling? We have no idea what could possibly be going wrong with this! That's why you're needed here! *'''Zenam:''' We don't have much time until full meltdown! There is an entrance to the power plant opposite the bank, go there and meet with the [[Head Engineer|Head Engineer]]! == Stage 2 == » Enter the sewers opposite of the bank of [[Llevigar]]. {{RenderLocation|location=Sewer Entrance|x=-1997|y=76|z=-4508}} Press the button to drop all the way down into the sewers. == Stage 3 == » Find the [[Head Engineer|head engineer]] at the [[Llevigar Power Plant|Llevigar power plant]] *The crackling and huming gets louder.. {{Location|location=[[Llevigar Power Plant]]|x=-2057|y=4|z=-4498}} [[File:HeadEngineer.png|200px|left]] '''Dialogue:''' *'''Head Engineer:''' Whew, finally Zeman found a guy for the job. I told him weeks ago this was a two-person task... *'''Head Engineer:''' Wait, it's Zenam? Eh, what does it matter? You knew who I was talkin' about. Lemme give you the lowdown real fast. *'''Head Engineer:''' About a month ago, there was a big blackout, and the generator here started acting up. Power surges, appliances overloading...heck, some of our lights turned purple! *'''Head Engineer:''' My grandfather and his engineers jury-rigged the whole system. A reset should fix all this, but as a security measure, I need to be here to oversee the reset. *'''Head Engineer:''' So that's your job- get through the system and find the reset button. These holes'll lead you around the generator rooms. Better get going! == Stage 4 == » Try to find a way into the machine {{HideSpoiler| Right next to the [[Head Engineer]], there is a short staircase with colored carpets on it leading to a hole in the wall. Go through the hole to get on top of the machine, then drop down to another hole with gray and blue carpets leading to it, and go through that one as well. You will be dropped into a tube; go all the way through it to reach the next section. |how to get into the machine}} == Stage 5 == » Find a way to proceed to the inner parts of the machinery *You can hear a soft humming coming from the walls. {{HideSpoiler|1= Moving block puzzle: * Try to get an iron block to the bottom center, and a gold block on the top center. * Make sure to get the gold block on the bottom right. * Once you have that, try to shift the blocks around until you get the pattern. * The colored buttons correspond to the sticky pistons on each side. * See this [https://youtu.be/oLmJoRoeuuw?t=4m35s guide]. The steps: # RED # GREEN # RED # YELLOW # GREEN # YELLOW # RED # GREEN # RED # GREEN # YELLOW # GREEN # YELLOW # RED ''(credit to Red-Eyed-Knight)'' |2=the first puzzle}} *A door has been opened {{MobTable}} | [[File:Bug(HeartofLlevigar).png|100px]] | Bug | 42 | 650 | Crawl AI | - | - | - | [[Llevigar Power Plant]] |- |} == Stage 6 == » Reach the center of the restricted area *You feel powerful electricity rushing through your body as you enter the room. '''Tip:''' Look up and follow one of the colored glass lines to get to the center. == Stage 7 == » Find the reset button. {{HideSpoiler |The Second Puzzle: *This requires planning ahead like in the game, Flow. *Use the buttons on the walls to change the color of the wire *See the picture below for the solution: [[File:FlowPuzzle.png|400px]] |the second puzzle}} *A door has been opened Go through the opened door and jump on the large red button. *''The system has been restarted'' == Stage 8 == » Report back to the [[Head Engineer]] [[File:HeadEngineer.png|200px|left]] '''Dialogue:''' *'''Head Engineer:''' Alright, that should do it- Ah, you're back. The system reset just fine, like I said. Everything should be winding down now. *'''Head Engineer:''' It's purging the excess energy now- might have a few more power surges and crackles for a while, but it'll go back to normal soon enough. *'''Head Engineer:''' Hm? What, there was a bug in the system? ...what, are you asleep or something? Of course there was a bug, we just fixed it! *'''Head Engineer:''' Well, anyways, Zebra should have our payment all settled. You go on ahead- I still need to stay down here to make sure everything stays smooth. *'''Head Engineer:''' ...oh, it's Zenam? D'you really care about getting his name right, or what? Anyways, the exit's through the water cooling system, it hooks up to the sewer exit. {{Clear}} == Stage 9 == » Ask [[Zenam]] for your reward [[File:Zenam.png|200px|left]] '''Dialogue:''' *'''Zenam:''' Whew...welcome back. The power is back online- Everything is running at full capacity again. I think the system is just running excess through right now. *'''Zenam:''' Glad you got here when you did though- some phony came through saying he was the expert. Clearly he was just here to take your contract money... *'''Zenam:''' Though, I was panicking so much I probably would've believed him... Speaking of which, here's your cut of the pay, and something extra for the trouble. *'''Zenam:''' Here's to hoping that the system won't run into any more trouble in the future. I don't want to be faced with an impending cataclysm like that again... {{Clear}} == Trivia == * The dialogue and special particle effects were updated in [[Version 1.20|1.20]]. * Near the end of the quest, right before talking to the [[Head Engineer]] again, you can see an endermite near the Power Core in the Power Plant, indicating that the reactor meltdown could have been caused by the endermite, being a literal bug in the system. ** This endermite is likely the same type as the strange creature that appears in [[Lexdale Witch Trials]], as the two creatures have similar behavior, such as burrowing into the ground and leaving dark spots behind. * In [[Rise of the Quartron]], Harnort's Notes mentioned that Zenam was actually a part of Project Quartron, but left due to an unknown reason. * This is the first [[Quests|Quest]] that takes place entirely in the province of [[Gavel Province|Gavel]]. * The real Electromagic Expert can be found at [-1867, 105, -4467], and will have the first of the following two dialogues before doing the quest and the second afterwards: ** '''Electromagic Expert:''' Aah, this is bad, where's the keys to my toolbox?! Zenam said it was important, I need to find them! ** '''Electromagic Expert:''' I still can't believe it, a human has done '''my''' job and taken '''my''' emeralds... Just because I lost my keys... {{Navbox Translations}} [[Category:Quests]]

pages.title: Blue Powder II Relic
pages.wikitext:
{{Removed|update = 1.17}}
{{Infobox/Town
| type = shrine
| image = [[File:Blue Powder II Relic Shrine.png|300px]]
| caption = The Blue Powder II Relic Shrine
| minlevel = 45
| mobs = Relic Guardian, Lv 45
| uses = Relic Shrine
| requirements = Touch the Relic Crystal
| otherinfo =
}}
The '''Blue Powder II Relic''' is one of 30 [[Relics]] scattered around the province of [[Wynn]] and [[Gavel]]. It is located within the Blue Powder II Relic Shrine. Collecting it gives one Water [[Powder]] II or I to the player, which can be added to weapons and armour.

==Location==
{{location|location=Outside [[Lusuco]]|x=-112|z=-285}}
Right outside the entrance to Lusuco, turn left onto the snow and gravel path. After passing under the arch, keep diagonally right, and you will find the shrine.

==Rewards==
When you touch the Relic Crystal, you will receive one of the following rewards:
{| class="wikitable"
! Reward
! Chance
|-
| {{WynnIcon|water powder i}} 1 x Water [[Powder]] II
| align="center" | ?%
|-
| {{WynnIcon|water powder i}} 1 x Water Powder I
| align="center" | ?%
|}
{{-}}
== Guardians ==
{{Infobox/Mob
| name = Relic Guardian
| image = [[File:Mob Iron Golem.png|200px]]
| type = Iron Golem
| level = 45
| location = Blue Powder II Relic
| drops =
| health = 2573
| damage = 150-175
| thunder = Weakness 
| water = Defense, Damage
| noCategory = true
}}
The Blue Powder II Relic is guarded by level 45 Iron Golems. They have no apparent advantages in terms of movement over other guard golems, but they have slightly more health than average level 45-50 mobs and a lot more damage.
[[Category:Relics]]

'''Drowning Squids''' are a [[Hostile Mobs (Wynn)|Hostile Mob]] found on the [[Coastal Trail]].
==Combat==
Drowning Squids are a melee enemy that follow the player and try to damage them by coming into contact with them.

They do not have any abilities.

==Drops==
Drowning Squids can drop the following items when defeated:
{{MobDrops}}
Drowning Squids may additionally drop [[Normal Items]], [[Unidentified Items]], [[Emeralds]], [[Powders]], and [[Potion of Healing|Potions of Healing]].
==Location==
Drowning Squids can appear all along Nemract's portion of the [[Coastal Trail]] alongside [[Sea Cockroach]]es and [[Dry Coral Reef]]s.

|x= -820
